relatedness_reasoning: Both files concern fraud/scam incidents and transaction holds; inbound reports fraud to risk_and_compliance, outbound notifies accounts of transaction holds — a direct causal pipeline. Multiple fields map clearly: customer identifiers, account details, transaction amounts, currency, country codes, severity, and risk indicators all correspond.

mappings.reasoning: System-generated unique identifier for the hold notice; no source in inbound.
Timestamp of notice generation likely derived from or close to the report date.
Hardcoded enum value 'Risk and Compliance' representing the issuing department; not derived from inbound data.
Hardcoded enum value 'Accounts' representing the destination department; not derived from inbound data.
Hardcoded enum value 'TRANSACTION_HOLD' representing the notice classification; not derived from inbound data.
Inbound severity 'high' is transformed to outbound enum 'HIGH' (case normalization).
Inbound customer.accountNumber (masked account) maps to outbound accountNumber; format differs (account_number to iban) but represents the same account identifier.
Direct copy of customer identifier from inbound to outbound.
Direct copy of customer name from inbound to outbound.
Hold reason combines fraud description and risk indicators to explain why the transaction hold was placed.
Hold effective date likely corresponds to incident detection date or shortly thereafter.
No inbound field specifies hold duration; likely determined by compliance policy or manual assessment.
Inbound transactionReference maps to outbound transactionId; same concept, different name.
Direct copy of fraudulent transaction amount from inbound to outbound.
Direct copy of currency code from inbound to outbound.
Inbound recipientCountry (country of fraudulent recipient) maps to outbound destinationCountry; same concept, different name.
Inbound transactionStatus 'blocked' is transformed to outbound enum 'HELD' (semantically equivalent hold state).
No quantified risk score in inbound; likely computed from riskIndicators or severity by compliance system.
Inbound riskIndicators array maps to outbound riskFlags array; same concept, different name.
Inbound actionsTaken describes past actions; outbound requiredActions describes future actions for Accounts department to resolve the hold.
No numeric escalation level in inbound; likely derived from severity or escalationRequired flag by compliance system.
Inbound escalationRequired flag indicates need for escalation; outbound approvalRequired indicates need for senior approval to release hold.
Hardcoded enum value 'Senior Compliance Officer'; not derived from inbound data.
Inbound additionalNotes maps to outbound notes; same concept, different name.
No expiry date in inbound; likely computed by adding hold duration to effective date or determined by policy.
Hardcoded enum value 'automated_compliance_system_v2.1' representing the issuing system; not derived from inbound data.
Inbound severity 'high' is transformed to outbound enum 'URGENT' (high severity maps to urgent priority).
